questions.question: In semiconductor manufacturing, what is the primary role of "photolithography"?
options: Depositing thin layers of metal for interconnects.
Transferring a geometric pattern from a mask to a wafer using light.
Removing unwanted material through chemical etching.
Implanting ions to change semiconductor conductivity.
Growing a protective oxide layer on the silicon surface.

questions.question: In digital IC design, what is the primary function of a "Phase-Locked Loop (PLL)"?
options: To perform high-speed analog-to-digital conversion.
To regulate the power supply voltage to different blocks.
To generate and synchronize clock signals at various frequencies and phases.
To provide electrostatic discharge (ESD) protection for I/O pins.
To buffer and amplify data signals between different logic gates.

questions.question: Which statement best describes a "load-store architecture" commonly found in RISC processors?
options: All instructions can operate directly on memory operands.
The CPU can execute multiple memory access operations in a single clock cycle.
Instructions are always fetched from a separate instruction memory and data from a separate data memory.
Only load and store instructions can access memory; arithmetic/logic operations work exclusively on registers.
The architecture supports variable-length instructions with complex addressing modes.

questions.question: In CMOS integrated circuits, what phenomenon is commonly referred to as "latch-up"?
options: A temporary short circuit between power and ground rails due to simultaneous switching noise.
The degradation of transistor threshold voltage over time due to hot carrier injection.
The unintended triggering of parasitic bipolar transistors, leading to a high-current path and potential device damage.
An error in the clock synchronization that causes a flip-flop to enter an unstable state.
The complete breakdown of a gate oxide layer due to excessive voltage stress.

questions.question: What is the primary architectural feature that allows a modern CPU to achieve "superscalar" performance?
options: Employing a very deep instruction pipeline (e.g., 20+ stages).
Integrating a powerful Graphics Processing Unit (GPU) on the same die.
Executing multiple instructions in parallel using multiple independent execution units.
Having a dedicated cache for each processing core.
Using a larger register file to hold more intermediate results.
